construct_description: The ability to explore, interpret, and draw sound conclusions from pharmacy dispensing and medication error data — including recognizing patterns in fill accuracy and error rates, questioning operational root causes, and translating analysis into actionable recommendations for patient safety improvement.

pba_category.name: Process/Demonstration Assessment
pba_category.description: Tasks that assess the process of problem-solving, not just the final answer. The system observes and logs how you investigate.

dimensions.name: Exploration
dimensions.description: Do you examine the data broadly before jumping to conclusions?

dimensions.name: Pattern Recognition
dimensions.description: Can you identify error spikes, outliers, and meaningful patterns across locations?

dimensions.name: Hypothesis Testing
dimensions.description: Do you test your ideas against the data rather than assuming?

dimensions.name: Interpretation & Recommendation
dimensions.description: Can you translate findings into clear, evidence-backed patient safety recommendations?

dataset.name: Meridian Pharmacy Group — Dispensing Quality Report Q1–Q4
dataset.description: Fill accuracy, medication error reports, and operational data across 4 pharmacy locations. One location had a significant error spike in Q2 — figure out which location, what likely caused it, and what to recommend.
insight_hints: Store 02 Westside had a dramatic Q2 spike: error rate jumped from 2.1 to 7.8 per 1,000 fills
Store 02 Q2 tech hours dropped from 720 to 480 — a 33% staffing reduction
Store 02 Q2 pharmacist hours also dropped by 33% (360 to 240)
Store 02 Q2 wait time nearly doubled (12 to 28 min) — patients waiting longer than usual
Store 02 Q3 staffing and error rate returned to near-baseline — suggesting the Q2 issue was temporary
Store 04 Lakeview has consistently elevated error rates (~3.1–3.4) and wait times — a structural capacity issue, not a spike
The staffing-to-error-rate correlation is strong: the only major error spike coincides exactly with major staffing reduction
Fill volume at Store 02 actually increased slightly in Q2 despite the staffing cut — amplifying the error risk

task.title: Meridian Pharmacy Group — Q2 Quality Review
task.briefing:
Meridian Pharmacy Group's Quality & Compliance team is reviewing dispensing accuracy data across its four locations after receiving a flagged incident report from Q2. Leadership knows something went wrong in Q2 — but they want your analysis to confirm which location, identify the likely operational cause, and recommend corrective action before the state board audit.

Your job: explore the dispensing data, identify the Q2 quality breakdown, determine what the data suggests caused it, and write a brief recommendation.
task.writing_prompt: Based on your analysis, write a 150–250 word memo to Meridian Pharmacy Group leadership identifying: (1) which location had the most significant Q2 quality issue, (2) what the data suggests caused it, (3) the patient safety impact, and (4) your recommended corrective action.
